text:
IN THE HIGH COURT OF KARNATAKA AT
BENGALURU
Civil Appeal No. 1234 of 2023
Date of Judgment: 15 March 2024
Between:
Mr. Rajesh Kumar (Appellant)
Vs.
ABC Finance Pvt Ltd (Respondent)
Coram:
Hon'ble Justice S. Narayan
JUDGMENT
1. Facts of the Case:
The appellant had taken a personal loan from the respondent company.
Due to financial hardship, the appellant defaulted on EMIs for three months.
The respondent initiated recovery proceedings and imposed a penalty charge of INR 75,000.
2. Issues for Consideration:
Whether the penalty imposed by the respondent was excessive and unfair.
Whether the recovery proceedings violated principles of natural justice.
3. Arguments:
The appellant argued that the penalty was arbitrary and disproportionate.
The respondent contended that the penalty was as per the loan agreement terms.
4. Findings and Reasons:
Upon examining the loan agreement, this Court finds

spondent contended that the penalty was as per the loan agreement terms.
4. Findings and Reasons:
Upon examining the loan agreement, this Court finds that the penalty clause
allowed for reasonable compensation but not excessive charges.
The imposed penalty appears disproportionate to the actual loss suffered.
5. Decision:
The appeal is partly allowed.
The penalty of INR 75,000 is reduced to INR 20,000.
The recovery proceedings shall continue subject to this modification.
6. Legal Provisions Referred:
Indian Contract Act, 1872 (Section 74)
Principles of Natural Justice
Order:
The respondent shall refund the excess penalty amount within 30 days.
Sd/-
Justice S. Narayan
